title: Buying in the Coachella Valley: Why the Season You Shop Matters
excerpt: Desert real estate runs on a seasonal clock. Understanding it can change what you pay, how much competition you face, and what's even on the market.
content:
The Coachella Valley market has a rhythm unlike almost anywhere else in California. From roughly November through April — "the season" — the valley fills with seasonal residents and visitors, open houses are busy, and well-priced homes in popular communities can move quickly.

## What that means for buyers

Shopping in season means maximum selection: sellers list when the audience is here. But it also means more competition, especially for turnkey homes in golf and resort communities.

Summer tells a different story. Inventory thins, but the buyers who remain are serious — and so are the sellers still listed in July. Negotiating room tends to widen when it's 110 degrees outside.

## Three practical takeaways

1. If selection matters most, shop December through March and be ready to move decisively.
2. If price matters most, the off-season can reward patience — fewer bidders, more motivated sellers.
3. Either way, know the community: HOA rules, rental policies, and land-lease vs. fee-simple status change the math more than the month does.

Every community in the valley has its own personality and its own numbers. Market data — median prices, days on market, active inventory — tells you which way a neighborhood is leaning before you ever step inside a house.

title: HOA Basics: What Desert Community Fees Actually Cover
excerpt: HOA fees in the valley range from under $100 to well over $1,000 a month. The difference isn't arbitrary — it's about what the community carries for you.
content:
Few numbers on a desert listing raise more questions than the HOA fee. In the Coachella Valley the spread is enormous — and usually rational once you see what's included.

## The low end

Sub-$100 fees typically cover common-area landscaping and little else. You own your roof, your walls, and your utility bills.

## The middle

In many gated communities, $300–$600 a month buys guarded or controlled access, community pools and spas, and sometimes cable and internet bundles. Condo associations here often carry roofs and exterior walls too — worth confirming, because it changes your insurance needs.

## The high end

Country-club communities can run $700 and up, and the fee may bundle amenities that would cost far more a la carte: fitness centers, tennis and pickleball, social calendars. Note that golf is usually a separate membership — don't assume the fee includes the course you're looking at.

## Questions worth asking on any HOA home

1. What exactly does the fee cover — and what's the assessment history?
2. Are short-term or seasonal rentals allowed, and with what minimums?
3. Is the land fee-simple or leased? Land-lease homes price differently for a reason.

An HOA is a shared contract with your neighbors. Read it like one.

title: Pool Homes in the Desert: What They Really Cost to Own
excerpt: A private pool is close to standard in some valley communities — but ownership carries real monthly costs worth budgeting before you fall in love.
content:
In the Coachella Valley, a private pool isn't a luxury add-on so much as part of the standard equipment in many neighborhoods. But "has a pool" and "budgeted for a pool" are different things.

## The recurring costs

Weekly service in the valley commonly runs $120–$200 a month depending on the pool and the season. Electricity for the pump adds more — variable-speed pumps help substantially. If the pool is heated for winter swimming, expect the gas bill to notice: heating a pool in January is a real line item.

## The occasional costs

Plaster or pebble resurfacing comes due every 10–15 years. Equipment — pumps, heaters, salt cells — has its own replacement cycle. A pre-purchase pool inspection is inexpensive insurance against inheriting a resurfacing bill in your first year.

## The other side of the ledger

Pools carry their weight here. For seasonal living they're central to the lifestyle buyers come for, and in the rental market — where community rules allow it — a private pool is often the single most requested feature.

Budget honestly, inspect before you buy, and the pool becomes what it should be: the best room in the house from May to October.
